ACT ONE FADE IN? Scene | EXT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY - AFTERNOON We see a handpainted sign reading "Springfield Elementary School Carnival - The Happiest Place on Earth". PAN DOWN to PRINCIPAL SKINNER being confronted by Mr. Burns’ LAWYER from "Bart Gets Hit by a Car", and TWO GOONS. The lawyer has a mouse-ears logo on the side of his briefcase. LAWYER Principal Skinner, "The Happiest Place on Earth" is a registered Disneyland copyright. SKINNER (GENIAL) Gentlemen, it’s just a small school carnival. LAWYER And it’s heading for a great big lawsuit. (JABS SKINNER IN CHEST) You made a big mistake, Skinner. SKINNER

(STILL GENIAL) Well, so did you.

You got an ex-Green Beret mad.

9F02 REVISED TABLE DRAFT 3/27/92 2. Scene!

co With lightning quickness, Skinner drives two fingers into

the neck of one of the goons and delivers a martial arts-— type kick to the lawyer’s solar plexus. They fall to the ground writhing and moaning. The second goon runs away. Skinner picks up the briefcase and throws it at the receding figure. After a beat it hits the goon. Skinner walks off adjusting his tie and smoothing his hair. SKINNER (CONT’D) (A LA JAMES BOND) Copyright expired. ANGLE - BOOTH A row of kids, including NELSON and MARTIN, are poised at the carnival game where you shoot water into a clown’s mouth, inflating a balloon. BARKER Annnd...begin! Kids point their water pistol at the clowns. NELSON This sucks. Nelson points his gun at Martin and sprays him in the ear. MARTIN (SCREAMS) NELSON

Haw haw!

BART is operating a three-card monte game. A small crowd is watching hin.

BART Pick the red, get ahead, pick the black, set you back.

Skinner walks up.

Scare|

SKINNER ceo wt I don’t recall authorizing this booth. BART Goodbye, gentlemen. Bart drops a pellet on the ground. It bursts into a smoke screen on contact, obscuring everyone’s vision. When the

smoke clears, Bart and the table are gone.

ANGLE - GROUNDSKEEPER WILLIE’S STAND = s Cerne

WILLIE Get yer haggis, right here! Chopped heart and lungs, boiled in a wee sheep’s stomach! Tastes as good as it sounds. Get it, while the gettin’s good. Nobody approaches. Willie SIGHS. ANGLE - A "GUESS YOUR AGE AND WEIGHT" BOOTH BOOTH MAN I’d say...53 years old and 420 pounds. PULL BACK to see he is looking at Homer. HOMER Ha, ha, you lose! 36 and 239! ANGLE - “THE PARALYZER"

Otto operates a creaky thrill ride that whips kids around at dangerously high speeds.

9F02 REVISED TABLE DRAFT 3/27/92 4. Scone 2 BART Cont Hey, Otto-man. I dare you to make this go faster. oTTo Challenge accepted! He pulls the lever all the way back, accelerating the ride to suicidal speed. An empty capsule breaks loose and flies through the air, CRASHING into the school and BURSTING into flames. OTTO (CONT’D) (TO BART) I’11 be in Mexico ‘til this thing blows over. He exits pursued by an angry, YELLING crowd. ANGLE - SPOOKHOUSE

A hand-painted sign outside a shack reads "Jimbo’s Spookhouse". Milhouse approaches in his scout uniform.

MILHOUSE Oh boy! A spookhouse! He goes in. INT. SPOOKHOUSE The inside of the shack is bare. Jimbo, Dolph and Kearney are there. Dolph and Kearney grab Milhouse by the kerchief. KEARNEY Hand over all your money. JIMBO (HITS MILHOUSE IN THE STOMACH)

The bullies take the money and shove Milhouse out the door. Bart walks up to hin.

BART

Was it scary?

9F02 REVISED TABLE DRAFT 3/27/92 5. Save L MILHOUSE Conk (IN PAIN) Uh-huh. BART Oh baby! Bart goes in the spookhouse. BART (V.0.) Uh-oh. EXT. - CARICATURIST’S BOOTH = EVENING ~ Se 3 Lisa is at a booth, over which is a sign reading "Caricatures $3". The artist’s drawings are displayed on an overhead projector. There are some dog-eared caricatures of Farrah Fawcett-Majors, the Fonz and a surfing Darth Vader. CARICATURIST

(WISE GUY VOICE) So, you like

roller-skating? LISA No. CARICATURIST

(DRAWING, OBLIVIOUS) Everybody loves roller-skating. OK you can turn around now.

Lisa turns around expectantly and GASPS.

ANGLE - CARICATURE

Projected on the screen is a very goofy, unflattering likeness of Lisa.

The caricaturist has drawn her roller-skating, chasing after a horrified-looking boy, in the standard big-head- little-body theme park style. The crowd LAUGHS.

ANGLE - LISA

She looks stunned.

CARNIVAL - LATE AFTERNOON Skinner is at the microphone. Homer, Marge and Lisa are in the crowd. Lisa is clutching her caricature. She looks at it mournfully. SKINNER In a moment, we’ll be raffling off our grand prize...

He unveils a posterboard on an easel, showing the Duff Blimp.

SKINNER (CONT’D) A ride on the famous Duff Beer Blimp! ANGLE - HOMER HOMER (DREAMILY) A ride on the Duff Blimp... DISSOLVE TO: HOMER’S FANTASY Sux 4 He’s riding in the cabin of the blimp, next to a PILOT. PILOT See the circular pattern on those fields? That’s from central-pivot

irrigation.

Ho- END FANTASY HOMER (CHANTING TO HIMSELF) Ho-mer, Ho-mer. SKINNER But first, our second-prize winner, and the recipient of this handsome shoe buffer....(DRAWS NAME OUT OF DRUM) ...Ned Flanders!

Flanders SLAPS his cheeks and goes up to collect his prize as the crowd APPLAUDS.

HOMER (WHINING) It’s no fair. We’1ll never have a buffer.

MARGE We have one at home. You never use it.

HOMER

Well, I want that one.

9F02 REVISED TABLE DRAFT 3/27/92 8. Swoxry SKINNER Cont And the winner of the blimp ride is... (READS TICKET) Homer Simpson! HOMER (TOO BIG) Oh my God! INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LISA’S BEDROOM - NIGHT — Scone S Lisa sits on her bed, miserably staring at the caricature. LISA'S POV

The horrified BoY in the caricature comes to life, as Lisa skates after hin.

CARICATURE BOY (SCREAMS) Terrified, he runs off the paper onto the floor and dives into a mouse hole. Lisa turns her mirror around so it faces the wall, and buries her face in the pillow. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - HALL - CONTINUOUS Homer walks down the hall. HOMER (TO THE TUNE OF "GEORGIE GIRL") Hey there Blimpy Boy Flying through the sky so fancy free.

Homer passes Lisa’s room and notices Lisa has her face buried in the pillow.

HOMER (CONT’D) (SOFTLY) Honey, what’s wrong? LISA Dad, do you think I’m ugly? HOMER

What are you talking about?

Scones tent

Without lifting her head, Lisa hands Homer the caricature. (The boy is back on.) HOMER (CONT’D) Heh heh heh...Oh, Lisa, this isn’t real. It’s just how you might look if you were a cartoon character. LISA I’m an ugmo. HOMER That’s not true! You’re cute as a bug’s ear. LISA Fathers have to say that stuff. Grampa walks by the door. HOMER Dad, am I cute as a bug’s ear? GRAMPA No! Homer tries to think of something to say, but can’t. He

walks out, defeated. A = Seon INT. MOE’S TAVERN - NIGHT

Homer sadly sits at the bar. He thoughtfully drags his finger through some spilled beer.

HOMER Hey, Moe, have you ever felt

unattractive?

They approach the FRENCH OWNER, a short man who wears a long pony tail and a fur cowboy hat. He turns and sees Marge’s hair. OWNER Mon dieu! You’ve come not a moment too soon. Hector, cancel my appointments. MARGE Well actually, I’m here for my daughter. OWNER (SNIDE) Of course -- how could one improve on that stately totem pole of yours? INT. SALON - LATER Lisa’s hair is clipped back with little pieces of foil. She’s wearing a masque and has cucumber slices over her eyes. FLIP THE PICTURE to show that Lisa’s hanging upside down. LISA Are we done? OWNER We are just warming up.

He fires up a BLOWTORCH and starts to flame cut Lisa’s hair.

QUICK CUTS of Lisa with different hairstyles: a page-boy, Little Orphan Annie, a beehive (she looks a little

nonplussed), and finally settling on her normal hair style, but each point comes to a little curl. She looks pleased.

9F02 REVISED TABLE DRAFT 3/27/92 28. Sou it ANGLE - BACKSTAGE

Homer is backstage with Lisa, who is holding her saxophone. Next to them is a tub of Vaseline.

HOMER They’11 love you just as much as I do. LISA Thanks, Dad. She quickly spreads some Vaseline on her teeth, smiles a little too big and walks out. After a moment, Homer dips his finger in the Vaseline and samples it. HOMER (NOT BAD) Hn.

As we CUT AWAY, Homer sticks his hand in the tub and grabs a big gob.

ANGLE - ONSTAGE Lisa takes center stage. LISA Some people say that to love your country is old fashioned, uncool, real melvin...
